mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer demand and construction activity, boosting corporate revenue forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, with a directional accuracy below 45% over a 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which improves short-term corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day SPX returns during early-cycle phases, with a mean forward return below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces monetary authorities to tighten policy expectations, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 60-day returns remain positive in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL rises > 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, increasing household income and consumer confidence, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS increase and UNRATE decrease signal yields a directional accuracy below 40% for 20-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product indicates stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate sales growth, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns (p-value > 0.10) over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a contraction in manufacturing output and weakening business investment, reducing near-term earnings expectations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances following two consecutive monthly INDPRO declines.
